gold_answers.question: Definition and importers of makeSuite
required_facts: makeSuite is defined at evals/lib/test-kit.mjs:10 as `export function makeSuite(importMetaUrl, { depth = 2 } = {})`

Exactly 12 files call makeSuite(...): evals/debug/contract-test.mjs, evals/new-feature/contract-test.mjs, evals/install/installer-safety-test.mjs, evals/project-setup/completion-state-test.mjs, evals/project-setup/contract-test.mjs, evals/project-setup/resume-test.mjs, evals/project-setup/commit-gate-test.mjs, evals/next-step-improve/contract-test.mjs, evals/next-step-improve/secrets-test.mjs, evals/next-step-improve/reconciliation-test.mjs, evals/next-step-improve/index-test.mjs, evals/next-step-improve/evidence-test.mjs

gold_answers.question: Call path runTests -> discoverTests -> nested walk -> readdirSync
required_facts: runTests is defined at scripts/run-deterministic-tests.mjs:37 and defaults its `tests` parameter to discoverTests()
discoverTests is defined at scripts/run-deterministic-tests.mjs:11 and declares a nested function walk at line 13
walk calls readdirSync(directory, { withFileTypes: true }).sort(...) at scripts/run-deterministic-tests.mjs:14

gold_answers.question: Tests/suites affected by changing CRLF normalization in makeSuite.read
required_facts: The CRLF normalization lives in the `read` accessor makeSuite returns: `readFileSync(join(root, path), "utf8").replace(/\r\n/g, "\n")` at evals/lib/test-kit.mjs:15

Every one of the 12 makeSuite(import.meta.url) consumers is a candidate for impact: evals/debug/contract-test.mjs, evals/new-feature/contract-test.mjs, evals/install/installer-safety-test.mjs, evals/project-setup/{completion-state-test.mjs,contract-test.mjs,resume-test.mjs,commit-gate-test.mjs}, evals/next-step-improve/{contract-test.mjs,secrets-test.mjs,reconciliation-test.mjs,index-test.mjs,evidence-test.mjs}

gold_answers.question: Validation entry points and their distinct responsibilities
required_facts: scripts/validate.sh: orchestrator gate — runs `node scripts/validate.mjs`, then the official Agent Skills spec validator (agentskills/skills-ref) against each skills/*/
scripts/validate.mjs: repo format gate — Agent Skills spec compliance + suite conventions (no external deps)
scripts/check-encoding.sh: UTF-8 integrity gate — round-trip self-test plus cp1252-mojibake signature scan
scripts/secrets-check.sh: scans the staged git diff (added lines only) for credential patterns before commit
scripts/run-deterministic-tests.mjs: discovers and runs every evals/**/*-test.{mjs,py} suite
scripts/run-routing-evals.mjs: trigger-routing evals mapping skill-name keywords/aliases to catalogMap entries

gold_answers.question: The skill vendoring rule and why a root-only adapter is invalid
required_facts: installed skills must be self-contained: install.sh copies skills/* alone to user scope (~/.claude/skills/*, ~/.agents/skills/*)
anything a skill executes at runtime must live inside its own scripts/ directory
repo-root scripts/ holds only the canonical copies (plus this repo's own dev tooling) and is never copied to user scope

therefore a shared executable adapter living only at the repository root would not exist once a skill is installed standalone; canonical-script changes must be re-vendored into each skill, checked by the format gate

gold_answers.question: Where is Postgres transaction handling? (unsupported)
required_facts: The repository contains no Postgres or transaction-handling content; the correct answer is an explicit abstain/not-found response, never a fabricated citation

gold_answers.question: Definition and direct test reference for enqueue
required_facts: enqueue is defined at src/queue.js:1 as `export function enqueue(queue, item) { queue.push(item); return queue.length; }`
test/queue.test.js imports enqueue at line 3 and exercises it in a test at line 4

gold_answers.question: Find queueSize after it is added and committed to src/queue.js, without a clean rebuild
required_facts: After committing `export function queueSize(queue) { return queue.length; }` as line 2 of src/queue.js (commit 25a57f9344669080fbb2b5e532e6e250f2cb096b), queueSize must be discoverable citing src/queue.js:2 without any index reset/rebuild step

gold_answers.question: Initial generated docs contain the enqueue signature and return behavior with a source citation
required_facts: A generated/maintained page states the enqueue(queue, item) signature
The page states enqueue appends one item and returns the queue length
The page cites src/queue.js:1

gold_answers.question: After F2, the same generated page also documents queueSize with a citation and retains the correct enqueue fact
required_facts: docs/queue.md documents the exact queueSize(queue) signature
docs/queue.md states queueSize returns the current queue length
docs/queue.md cites src/queue.js:2
docs/queue.md still correctly documents enqueue (no regression)

trials.version: n/a (hand-maintained Markdown, committed as repository content)

runs.notes: generated_page = docs/queue.md; enqueue signature, return-behavior, and citation all present at commit 221b9aa (docs: add queue API baseline)

FAIL as expected: docs/queue.md was never told to regenerate after F2, so it retains the correct enqueue fact but has zero mention of queueSize/src/queue.js:2. This is not a bug in the native path -- it demonstrates the exact gap a maintained-docs *provider* would need to close, and is why the documentation role stays native-only pending an evaluated provider.

files_written: docs/queue.md (committed once, manually, before any provider run; never regenerated)
